{{Quote|I went to the Imperial Opera and treated myself to their production of ''The Agony of Tarkin''. Spectators proved more amusing than the theatre at times.|Mara Jade|<ref name="mj" />}}
'''''The Agony of Tarkin''''' was an [[Opera/Legends|opera]] performed by the [[Coruscant Opera/Legends|Imperial Opera Company]].<ref name="mj" /> It was about the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], who perished aboard the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|I||''Agony of Tarkin, The''}}</ref>

The opera was running on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] during [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. The production used a huge stage, glowing in [[Color/Legends|pink]] light, and a stellar backdrop. It also allowed attendees in balcony seats to view close-up shots of the performance via [[Hologram/Legends|holograms]].<ref name="mj" />

During this period, [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] treated herself to a viewing of ''The Agony of Tarkin'' during a vacation from her duties as [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]]. She felt that at times, spectators were more amusing than the theatre. After the opera's conclusion, Jade attended a reception along with other guests to the opera, many of whom were [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Officer/Legends|officers]].<ref name="mj" />

==Behind the scenes==
''The Agony of Tarkin'' first appeared briefly in the [[Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand 2|second issue]] of ''[[Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand]]'', written by [[Timothy Zahn]] and [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and published in [[1998]]. It also received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[December]] [[2008]].
